methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $127M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Cambium is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Simon Waddington and Stephan Herrera that has raised $127M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company in the robotics sector, with particular focus on 6 same-stage Series B companies.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $127M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, yielding $241M. The peer-median method references six same-sector, same-stage comparables—1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and one undisclosed peer—producing a median valuation of $1.1B and a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $205M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $658M. The wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity within the Series B robotics cohort, driven primarily by differentiation in commercialization progress and market applicability. Key uncertainties include the relative maturity of Cambium's technology relative to outlier peers like 1X Technologies, and whether earlier-stage characteristics align more closely with the $300M–$500M band or higher-valuation comparables.
